goal: Projectiles and terrain deformation are synchronized in multiplayer: projectile spawns are replicated from the host, crater results are host-authoritative deterministic events, and all peers query the same deformed terrain heights and normals for movement and targeting.
non_goals: Projectile lag compensation or rewind/replay reconciliation
Full late-join match recovery beyond deformation replay request
Advanced armor deflection or directional damage
constraints: Prefer node/scene/Inspector configuration; keep runtime code only for behavior that cannot be represented as nodes.
Do not sync Terrain3D data, mesh buffers, collision buffers, images, or large binary state.

sync_contract.terrain_seed: Host includes the seed in the existing match setup RPC.
sync_contract.projectile_spawn: Host broadcasts primitive spawn data for visible projectiles: position, direction, speed, and damage.
sync_contract.ordering: Events are applied in host order. Fingerprint is seed/settings plus ordered crater events.
sync_contract.authority: Only server/host authors projectile gameplay effects and crater events while a multiplayer peer is active. Client projectiles are visual/simulation followers.
sync_contract.replay: Clients can request the current crater event list from the host and rebuild locally.

slices.acceptance: Plan records concrete scope, stop rules, sync contract, and verification gates.
Runbook accurately describes the MP deformation gate and leaves local-only v0.2.1 history intact.
slices.notes: Added v0.2.2 operator docs for host-authoritative primitive crater-event sync, automatic tank movement/targeting response via deformed terrain height/normal queries, and manual seed plus crater fingerprint comparison.

slices.acceptance: TerrainWorld can sanitize, emit, apply, clear, and replay primitive crater events deterministically.
Host broadcasts projectile spawns and crater events reliably; clients request/replay deformation state after match start; client projectiles do not author terrain craters or gameplay damage.
Audit identifies any missing smoke assertions or likely GDScript/runtime failure points before commit.
slices.notes: Read-only audit recommended untyped RPC arrays, no call_local double-apply, replay after seed setup, visual-only projectile safety, and local replay/idempotence smoke assertions.

slices.acceptance: Runtime smoke proves crater replay/fingerprint behavior and the match scene still starts.
slices.notes: Godot headless runtime smoke passed: crater replay fingerprint matched, visual projectile did not change terrain, projectile crater and core tank flow still passed.

tests: GDScript parse/load of changed scenes and scripts through Godot CLI.
Manual two-machine gate: host starts match, fires a projectile, client sees a synced projectile and the same crater, and tank slowdown/aim blocking follows the deformed terrain.
stop_rules: Stop if another agent edits outside its declared scope.
Stop if Godot reports a parse/runtime error that cannot be corrected within this slice.
